question.content: For the right-side modified resistor network (with added diagonal branches) and all segment resistances equal to r, compute the equivalent resistance between its two external terminals. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: nodal-voltage method. Set terminal B as 0 V, inject a test current I at A, and solve node potentials of the modified graph.
step_1.key_point: Use KCL equations instead of series-parallel visual collapsing.

step_3.content: Use mirror constraints only to merge equal-potential nodes, then write reduced KCL equations for the independent nodes.
step_3.key_point: Reduction is performed in equation space, giving a compact linear system.

step_5.content: Compute the terminal drop V_AB from the solved node voltages and evaluate R_eq = V_AB/I = 2*r/3.
step_5.key_point: Test-current extraction directly yields the equivalent resistance.
